content: /agentic-engineering I'd like for us to put together a skill in managing github pages deployments grounded in april 2026 procedures so that we have the appropriate processes in place in advance. this will be a project level skill but scope it generally in terms of the research and design of the skill. i'd like dedicated agents, commands checklists, and refrence documentation. if we need to emplly any pre-requisites or dependencises taht's fine.
skip the two optional dependencies. this all looks great and is what i had envisioned.

<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Volumes-GridStore-Ronin-Advisory-Website/7d0c2980-4891-4944-8b33-b65d655ebd67/tasks/a496e303f7ca3703e.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Changelog for pages deployment layer" completed</summary>
<result>Excellent. The changelog now captures the theory shifts from this session. Here's what I added to the `[Unreleased]` section under `### Added`:

**Four new entries** (lines 20–23):

1. **First project-level skill** — Explains the new three-layer agentic vocabulary (skill=theory/staleness, agent=mechanics, command=entry). The shift from agents-only to skills+agents+commands changes how the project thinks about durable knowledge.

2. **GitHub Actions deployment surface** — Documents the operator-gated workflow with read-only rollback (advisor proposes, operator executes). Emphasizes continuation of the "never auto-commit" posture.

3. **Reference documentation pattern** — Captures the staleness-tracking design (dated specs with re-verify cadences). Addresses the April 2026 reality that GitHub action majors drift; future sessions can cold-start via the progressively-disclosed knowledge surface.

4. **`.nojekyll` significance** — Explains why this empty file is load-bearing and ties it to the specific `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v5` requirement (`include-hidden-files: true`).

Each entry answers "what changed about the project's shape?" rather than "which files were added." The reader rebuilding a theory cold three months from now learns:
- The agentic layer now has skills as a first-class pattern.
- Deployment is operator-controlled and has explicit pre-flight gates.
- GitHub action specs are versioned artifacts with staleness tracking.
- The `.nojekyll` file is not a casualty of the deployment system; it's a deliberate preservation mechanism for hand-written HTML.

**File changed:** `/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/CHANGELOG.md`</result>

<task-notification>
<task-id>a0764299e618bde1e</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01HiAdp1Acj4NMxkYWr57toM</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Volumes-GridStore-Ronin-Advisory-Website/7d0c2980-4891-4944-8b33-b65d655ebd67/tasks/a0764299e618bde1e.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Dev-docs update for pages layer" completed</summary>
<result>All three edits are clean and coherent. Here is what was changed and why nothing else was touched.

---

Three files were updated. No new files were created.

**`/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/dev/architecture.md`**

Two changes. First, the layer list was expanded from four to five entries. The workflow at `.github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml` is now layer 2 — the gate from commit to public URL — because a reader orienting to the project needs to know that artifact exists and what the `include-hidden-files: true` flag does before they edit anything under `site/`. The `.nojekyll` explanation was also moved into the `site/` layer description, where it belongs. The `.claude/` entry was updated to name `.claude/skills/` alongside commands and agents, since the pages-deploy skill is the first skill entry and it was previously invisible. Second, the "A visitor sees only layer 1" sentence was reworded to track the renumbered layers and to name layer 2's role explicitly.

**`/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/dev/contributing.md`**

One change. The sentence "Two exist today" and the plan list gained a third entry for `03-pages-deployment.md`. A future contributor reading this section needs to know plan 03 exists and what it introduced, because it is the canonical place to look when the workflow needs to be changed or the action versions need to be bumped.

**`/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/dev/visual-qa.md`**

One change. The "What this loop replaces" section previously stated "No CI gates today" as a bullet — that claim became false when the preflight job landed. The bullet was removed and replaced with a short paragraph that names the structural/visual distinction: CI gates prevent bad artifacts from reaching the CDN; the visual review loop catches what structural gates cannot see. The two concerns are complementary, not alternatives. A link to plan 03 was added so a reader who wants the preflight gate definitions knows where to go.

**`/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/dev/pages/README.md`** — left alone. It is an accurate, self-contained pointer and had no gaps.

**`/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/dev/tokens.md`**, **`typography.md`**, **`authoring.md`** — left alone. Deployment is not part of their concern domain.</result>

</task-notification>

<task-notification>
<task-id>ae7beef1695c86133</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01GWn7ERPxqpc46EGBr5HCyG</tool-use-id>
<output-file>/private/tmp/claude-501/-Volumes-GridStore-Ronin-Advisory-Website/7d0c2980-4891-4944-8b33-b65d655ebd67/tasks/ae7beef1695c86133.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Agent-docs update for pages layer" completed</summary>
<result>All 6 commands and 12 agents verified present. Here is what changed and why.

---

## What changed

### `/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/agent/agents.md`

Added a third section, **Deployment-pipeline agents**, between the quality-gate section and the dispatch-rules table. It documents the three new agents with the non-inferable theory:

- `rag-web-pages-preflight` and `rag-web-pages-verify` are mechanical — binary gates, no judgment, sonnet + effort:medium. The key non-obvious detail: `preflight` treats a missing binary (`lychee`, `actionlint`) as a hard failure, not a skip, because both are declared project prerequisites.
- `rag-web-pages-rollback-advisor` is advisory — judgment synthesis, opus + effort:high. The model/effort asymmetry with the other two is intentional and is the primary theory to transmit (it is not configuration drift; it is a deliberate cost/quality tradeoff for a high-stakes infrequent event).

The dispatch table gained three new rows for these agents and now explicitly states all three deployment-pipeline agents are `mirror_status: pending`.

### `/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/agent/commands.md`

Added a second major section, **Deployment commands — the `/rag-web-pages-*` group**, covering:

- The skill/command/agent layering (skill owns the why; commands own the when; agents are the instruments).
- Each command's contract, with the non-inferable invariants called out explicitly: the idempotency guard in `/rag-web-pages-init`, the dirty-tree hard abort in `/rag-web-pages-deploy`, the context-sensitive push vs `workflow_dispatch` selection, the ambiguity-surface behavior in `/rag-web-pages-rollback`.
- The name-collision known issue with a pointer to `conventions.md` for the full entry.

### `/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/agent/harness.md`

Extended the `pi-agents.yaml` schema section with two additions:

- The `skills:` bucket is now populated. Its `mirrors:` value points to a directory, not a file — a non-obvious schema distinction that matters when an agent tries to verify the entry.
- The `assets:` bucket semantics: it captures harness-agnostic files that are meaningful to the deployment lifecycle but carry no parity obligation. The four current asset entries are enumerated with their exemption rationale.

### `/Volumes/GridStore/Ronin-Advisory-Website/docs/agent/conventions.md`

Added two new sections before the `rag-web-*` namespace section:

1. **The skill layer** — theory of what a skill is relative to agents and commands, why the structure (SKILL.md + reference/ + templates/) exists, and why the alternative (embedding dated spec content in agent bodies) produces invisible staleness. Includes the Decision 5 quote from the plan as the warrant.

2. **Known issues / Name collision** — the `rag-web-pages-deploy` skill-vs-command display ambiguity, its cosmetic-not-functional status, and a concrete resolution path (rename the command to `/rag-web-pages-push`) if it becomes operationally confusing.

### `CLAUDE.md` — no changes

At 53 lines with no inventorial content and no new durable project-wide antipatterns exposed by the deployment work. The `include-hidden-files: true` / `.nojekyll` failure mode is deployment-domain-specific and already in `SKILL.md`'s Doctrine section and `reference/troubleshooting.md` — adding it to `CLAUDE.md` would be inventorial drift.</result>

<summary>Agent "Vault export of finalized docs" completed</summary>
<result>All 12 notes are confirmed in the vault. Here is the summary for the operator:

The export ran against the `Kensho-2026` Obsidian vault using the CLI at `/Applications/Obsidian.app/Contents/MacOS/obsidian`. Twelve notes were written to `Projects/rag-web/docs/` using the `rag-web - <title>.md` naming convention, each with the five-property YAML block (`project`, `doc-type`, `source`, `generated`, `tags`), Obsidian callouts wrapping key intent/warning sections, and wikilinks connecting the corpus. The set covers: the MOC index (`rag-web - Index.md`), all four agent-layer docs (agents, commands, conventions, harness), five dev docs (architecture, contributing, visual-qa, pages-deployment pointer, and plan 03), plus CLAUDE.md and the changelog. The index MOC is the designed landing point — it surfaces the session delta (GitHub Pages deployment layer), flags all pending Pi mirrors in a warning callout, and maps the full corpus by audience within two clicks. Every stdout line was scanned for `Error:` after each invocation; all 12 returned clean.</result>
